helpful: Provisioned petition/BIO full text — the SG's split recommendation (grant 25-1017, deny 25-1022) was the single most predictive fact and lived only in the BIO
Corpus query --include-open: surfaced the companion's date_cert_granted=2026-06-29, the decisive forward signal
statpack relist-count cut for calibrating the hold/relist pattern
gaps: fedcourts query cannot filter by docket number or case name — finding a named companion docket required pulling up to 1000 ranked rows and grepping; a --docket-number/--case-name filter would make companion lookups one call
CourtListener MCP has no docket entries for SCOTUS dockets (search and docket-entries both empty), so live SCOTUS order-list status has to come from the corpus or the open web
A provisioned pointer to companion/linked dockets (the snapshot's 'Links: 25A674' and the VIDED entries imply them) with their current status would have saved most of the retrieval budget
notes: Citation-filtered corpus query returned zero rows for heavily-cited authorities (570 U.S. 1, 555 U.S. 488); citation matching may be sparse for cert-stage dockets.
